steps.title: Compare mammal counts
steps.content: The researchers conclude that children may be more aware of mammals than of other animals in their environments. The supporting data should therefore show mammals appearing in more drawings than the other animal groups shown in the graph.

steps.title: Reject irrelevant data
steps.content: A compares insects with birds but not mammals, B misidentifies which group appears least, and C says insects exceed mammals, which would weaken the conclusion. The useful data show mammal examples appearing more often than bird or insect examples, directly matching the claim about greater awareness of mammals. The answer is D.
